book: English Vocabulary in Use Pre-intermediate and Intermediate

units.title: Learning vocabulary
units.topic: Study skills for learning and revising vocabulary

words.description: Something you do often and usually in the same way
words.example: It's a good idea to have a routine when you use this book.

words.description: Not less than a particular amount
words.example: Study a new unit for at least 30-45 minutes.

words.description: Happening or done once a week
words.example: A weekly routine when you study a new unit.

words.description: Happening or done every day
words.example: A daily routine when you revise that unit.

words.description: To study something again that you have already learned
words.example: You may only need to revise for five or ten minutes each time.

words.description: Doing things energetically rather than passively
words.example: When you are studying a unit for the first time, you need to be active.

words.description: To speak something so you can hear it
words.example: With a new word or phrase, say it aloud.

words.description: In your head, not speaking out loud
words.example: Repeat it to yourself silently.

words.description: A pen used to mark important text in bright colour
words.example: Use a highlighter pen to mark words you think are important.

words.description: To remove pencil marks using a rubber/eraser
words.example: You can rub them out (using a rubber) and do them again later.

words.description: A small object used to remove pencil marks
words.example: You can rub them out using a rubber.

words.description: To check what you remember without looking at the answer
words.example: Test yourself, e.g. look at a word and cover the meaning.

units.title: Keeping a vocabulary notebook
units.topic: Strategies for recording new vocabulary in a notebook

words.description: To record information in writing
words.example: I write down new English words and phrases in my notebook.

words.description: The conversion of words from one language to another
words.example: Next to each word I write a Spanish translation.

words.description: A state of being untidy or disorganised
words.example: My notebook is a mess. [nothing is in a good order]

words.description: To create a picture using a pen or pencil
words.example: I like to draw pictures.

words.description: To write down something so you remember it
words.example: I sometimes make a note of new words in my notebook.

words.description: To not remember something
words.example: I often forget. [don't remember]

words.description: Organised according to subject category
words.example: I try and list words by topic, so I put all the animals together.

words.description: To write down information briefly
words.example: I note down new words and phrases.

words.description: A description of what something means
words.example: I write an explanation in English if it is not difficult.

words.description: A piece of advice to help you with something
words.example: A tip is a piece of advice to help you.

words.description: To put things together without organisation, causing confusion
words.example: Don't mix words from different topics up.

words.description: Needed; required
words.example: Write a translation if it is necessary.

words.description: Not needed; not required
words.example: Don't write things that are unnecessary.

words.description: A word with the same or similar meaning to another word
words.example: awful (adjective) = terrible (synonym: dreadful)

words.description: Words that are commonly used together
words.example: Example sentences help you with word partners (collocations).

units.title: Using a dictionary
units.topic: How to use dictionaries effectively and common defining words

words.description: Using or able to use two languages
words.example: A bilingual dictionary is easy for you to understand.

words.description: To find a word's meaning in a dictionary
words.example: If you look up a word using the dictionary...

words.description: An explanation of the meaning of a word
words.example: A definition explains the meaning.

words.description: Symbols used to show the pronunciation of a word
words.example: Pronunciation using phonemic symbols.

words.description: A grammatical category of a word, e.g. noun, verb, adjective
words.example: [U] tells you that 'fun' is a noun.

words.description: To give something more attention and importance
words.example: My teacher has always emphasised the importance of writing down new words.

words.description: Having a relationship with someone or something
words.example: Musical is connected with / related to music.

words.description: How much there is of something
words.example: £5 million is a large amount of money.

words.description: Done by the government or someone in authority
words.example: A passport is an official document.

words.description: To do or say things in a particular way
words.example: People can behave strangely when they're nervous.

words.description: A letter in a word that is not pronounced
words.example: In the word island, the letter 's' is silent.

units.title: English language words
units.topic: Grammar terms: parts of speech, pronunciation and punctuation

words.description: A word used in place of a noun, e.g. I, you, he, she, it
words.example: I is a pronoun.

words.description: A word for a person, place or thing
words.example: Chair, window, morning and music are all nouns.

words.description: A word describing an action or state
words.example: Have, sit and listen are verbs.

words.description: A word that describes a noun
words.example: Brown and leather are adjectives.

words.description: A word that describes a verb, adjective or another adverb
words.example: Often is an adverb.

words.description: A word showing the relationship between a noun and other words, e.g. in, at, on
words.example: By and to are prepositions.

words.description: The word 'the', used for specific things
words.example: The is a definite article.

words.description: The word 'a' or 'an', used for non-specific things
words.example: A is an indefinite article.

words.description: A word that connects clauses or sentences, e.g. and, but, so
words.example: And is a conjunction or link word.

words.description: A noun that can be counted and has a plural form
words.example: Books, apples, chairs are countable nouns.

words.description: A noun that cannot be counted and has no plural form
words.example: Information, advice are uncountable nouns.

words.description: Following the standard grammar pattern, e.g. for past tense -ed
words.example: Work, live are regular verbs.

words.description: Not following the standard grammar pattern
words.example: Go/went, take/took are irregular verbs.

words.description: A verb combined with a preposition or adverb to make a new meaning
words.example: Take something off, wake up are phrasal verbs.

words.description: Letters added to the beginning of a word that change its meaning
words.example: In uncomfortable, un- is a prefix.

words.description: Letters added to the end of a word that change its meaning or form
words.example: -able is a suffix.

words.description: A unit of pronunciation with one vowel sound
words.example: Book has one syllable, before has two syllables.

words.description: The emphasis placed on a syllable when pronouncing a word
words.example: It is important to know which syllable to stress.

words.description: An uppercase letter used at the beginning of sentences and names
words.example: Every sentence must begin with a capital letter.

words.description: The punctuation mark '.' used at the end of a sentence
words.example: Every sentence must end with a full stop.

words.description: The punctuation mark ',' used to show a pause in a sentence
words.example: Some sentences have a comma.

words.description: The punctuation mark '?' used at the end of a question
words.example: A question must end with a question mark.

units.title: Country, nationality and language
units.topic: Countries, nationalities, languages and parts of the world

words.description: To be originally from a particular country
words.example: I come from Argentina, so I'm Argentinian.

words.description: The main city of a country where the government is based
words.example: The capital is Buenos Aires.

words.description: The number of people living in a place
words.example: The capital has a population of more than 10 million people.

words.description: The main official language of China
words.example: In China they speak Mandarin (and Cantonese).

words.description: The official language of Israel
words.example: In Israel they speak Hebrew.

words.description: One of the large land masses on Earth
words.example: The continents in the world are Europe, Africa, Asia, North America, South America, Australia and Antarctica.

words.description: A region including countries like the UAE and Saudi Arabia
words.example: The Middle East includes United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia.

words.description: A region including countries like Thailand and Japan
words.example: The Far East includes Thailand and Japan.

words.description: A region including countries like Jamaica and Barbados
words.example: The Caribbean includes Jamaica and Barbados.

words.description: The northern European region including Sweden, Norway, Denmark
words.example: Scandinavia includes Sweden, Norway, Denmark and Finland.

words.description: Used with 'the' to refer to a nationality group as a whole
words.example: The British / The French are...

words.description: Nationalities ending in -ian/-ian that take 's' for plural, no 'the' needed
words.example: Brazilians/Russians are...

units.title: The physical world
units.topic: Geographic features and facts about the physical world

words.description: The top or outside part of something
words.example: Two thirds of the surface of the Earth is covered in water.

words.description: The planet we live on
words.example: Two thirds of the surface of the Earth is covered in water.

words.description: Having a layer of something over the surface
words.example: The surface was covered in water; the ground was covered with snow.

words.description: How hot or cold something is
words.example: Temperatures of over 57 degrees Celsius have been recorded.

words.description: A unit of measurement for temperature
words.example: 57°C (57 degrees Celsius).

words.description: A large natural hole in the side of a hill or under the ground
words.example: The Krubera-Voronja Cave is the deepest cave in the world.

words.description: An artificial waterway for boats or irrigation
words.example: The Beijing-Hangzhou Grand Canal is the longest canal in the world.

words.description: A place where water falls from a height, e.g. over a cliff
words.example: The highest waterfall in the world is the Angel Falls.

words.description: A dense tropical forest with very high rainfall
words.example: The Amazon Rainforest is the largest rainforest in the world.

words.description: A very large area of sea
words.example: The Pacific is the largest ocean in the world.

words.description: To be made or formed from particular parts
words.example: Switzerland consists of three main geographical regions.

words.description: An area within a country or the world
words.example: Switzerland consists of three main geographical regions.

words.description: A big difference between two things
words.example: Switzerland is a land of contrasts.

words.description: The appearance of an area of land
words.example: Switzerland has completely different landscapes.

words.description: The general weather conditions of a place over a long period
words.example: The climate can change within a very short distance.

words.description: The amount of space between two places
words.example: The distance between the two is just 70 kilometres.

words.description: The four main directions of the compass
words.example: Ascona in the south has an almost Mediterranean climate.

words.description: The intermediate compass directions
words.example: The wind is coming from the north-west.

units.title: Weather
units.topic: Weather conditions, rain, storms and temperature

words.description: Having thick fog; difficult to see far
words.example: It was very foggy this morning.

words.description: Having a lot of clouds in the sky
words.example: It was very cloudy this morning, but the sun came out after lunch.

words.description: Covered in ice; very cold
words.example: The roads were icy this morning.

words.description: When the sun appears after being hidden by clouds
words.example: It was cloudy, but the sun came out after lunch.

words.description: Very dense fog that makes it hard to see
words.example: The accident happened in thick fog.

words.description: A large amount of rain falling
words.example: We had some heavy rain at the weekend.

words.description: A small amount of rain falling gently
words.example: Light rain is the opposite of heavy rain.

words.description: Wind blowing with a lot of force
words.example: There was a strong wind when we were on the boat.

words.description: When wind has caused movement of something
words.example: The wind has blown all the apples off the tree.

words.description: A sky with no clouds
words.example: The sky was clear by lunchtime. [no clouds]

words.description: To a very great degree; very
words.example: It's been extremely cold today.

words.description: To rain very heavily
words.example: It poured with rain this afternoon.

words.description: A short period of light or heavy rain
words.example: We had a couple of heavy/light showers this morning.

words.description: Heavy rain with strong winds
words.example: A storm is heavy rain with strong winds.

words.description: A storm with thunder and lightning
words.example: A period of hot weather sometimes ends with a thunderstorm.

words.description: When the air feels very warm and wet
words.example: First it becomes very humid.

words.description: The loud noise heard during a storm
words.example: You hear thunder and see lightning.

words.description: A bright flash of light in the sky during a storm
words.example: You hear thunder and see lightning.

words.description: Extremely hot
words.example: Boiling means very hot.

words.description: Having a pleasant, moderate heat
words.example: It's quite warm today.

words.description: Slightly cold, either pleasantly or unpleasantly
words.example: We've had a cool summer. / The water in the pool was lovely and cool.

words.description: Unpleasantly cold
words.example: It's a bit chilly today, take a jacket.

words.description: Extremely cold; below freezing point
words.example: Freezing means very cold.

words.description: Not as cold as usual; pleasantly moderate
words.example: It's been a mild winter.

units.title: Animals and insects
units.topic: Pets, farm animals, wild animals, insects and sea creatures

words.description: To have animals living with you at home
words.example: In the UK, many people keep pets.

words.description: A container with bars for keeping birds or small animals
words.example: Parrots are usually in a cage.

words.description: A very small rodent. Singular: mouse, plural: mice
words.example: Children sometimes keep mice (singular: mouse).

words.description: A small furry animal with long ears
words.example: Children sometimes keep rabbits.

words.description: A small amphibian with smooth skin that lives near water
words.example: Some people keep more unusual pets, e.g. frogs, snakes and spiders.

words.description: A long reptile with no legs
words.example: Some people keep snakes as pets.

words.description: A small eight-legged creature that spins webs
words.example: Some people keep spiders as pets.

words.description: A farm animal similar to a small horse with long ears
words.example: Farms in the UK may have donkeys.

words.description: A farm animal kept for milk and meat
words.example: Farms in the UK may have goats.

words.description: An adult male cow
words.example: Farms in the UK may have a bull.

words.description: An animal that lives naturally in the wild, not domesticated
words.example: The pictures show a number of wild animals.

words.description: Living freely in their natural environment
words.example: You may see these animals in the wild.

words.description: A place where wild animals are kept for public display
words.example: You will probably see them in a zoo.

words.description: Not often seen or found
words.example: Some animals, for example tigers, are now quite rare.

words.description: To keep safe from harm
words.example: It is important that we protect these endangered animals.

words.description: At risk of becoming extinct
words.example: We protect these endangered animals.

words.description: A small to medium-sized primate that climbs trees
words.example: Monkey is a wild animal.

words.description: A large desert animal with one or two humps
words.example: A camel can survive in the desert without water for a long time.

words.description: A large wild cat with a spotted coat
words.example: A leopard is a wild animal with spots.

words.description: A large heavy wild mammal with thick fur
words.example: A bear is a large wild animal.

words.description: The long nose of an elephant
words.example: The elephant uses its trunk to drink water.

words.description: A living thing, especially an animal
words.example: Many different creatures live in the sea.

words.description: A very large sea mammal
words.example: A whale is the largest sea creature.

words.description: An intelligent sea mammal known for jumping out of water
words.example: Dolphins are very intelligent sea creatures.

words.description: A large predatory fish with sharp teeth
words.example: Some sharks are dangerous to humans.

words.description: A sea creature with eight arms
words.example: An octopus has eight arms.

units.title: The body and movement
units.topic: Parts of the body and movements involving mouth, face and head

words.description: The part of the face above the eyebrows
words.example: She had a headache and rubbed her forehead.

words.description: The side of the face below the eyes
words.example: The baby had round, pink cheeks.

words.description: The soft edges around the mouth opening
words.example: Drink water if your lips feel dry.

words.description: The part of the face below the mouth
words.example: She rested her chin on her hand.

words.description: The chest area of a woman's body
words.example: Breast is a part of the female chest.

words.description: The joint in the middle of the arm
words.example: I bumped my elbow on the table.

words.description: The narrow part of the body between chest and hips
words.example: She has a very slim waist.

words.description: The area between the waist and top of the leg
words.example: She fell and broke her hip.

words.description: The part of the body that you sit on
words.example: He sat down on the bottom step.

words.description: The joint connecting the hand to the lower arm
words.example: She wears a watch on her wrist.

words.description: The back part of the foot
words.example: These shoes hurt my heel.

words.description: To take air into and out of the lungs
words.example: People breathe through their nose or mouth.

words.description: To take air into the lungs / to let air out of the lungs
words.example: You breathe in and out about 12-15 times a minute.

words.description: To smile in the direction of someone
words.example: They sometimes smile at people to be polite.

words.description: To laugh because something is funny
words.example: People laugh at things which are funny.

words.description: To produce tears, usually from sadness
words.example: People sometimes cry if they're very unhappy.

words.description: To move your head up and down to mean 'yes'
words.example: People in some countries nod their head to mean 'yes'.

words.description: To move your head from side to side to mean 'no'
words.example: People shake their head for 'no'.

words.description: To open your mouth wide and breathe in deeply, usually when tired
words.example: People often yawn when they're tired.

words.description: To grasp and shake someone's hand as a greeting
words.example: They shook hands with each other when they met.

words.description: To cross your arms over your chest
words.example: He folded his arms and waited.

words.description: To move your hand from side to side as a greeting or farewell
words.example: She waved to her friend across the street.

words.description: To clear your nose by blowing air through it into a tissue
words.example: She blew her nose into a tissue.

words.description: To arrange your hair using a comb
words.example: She combs her hair every morning.

units.title: Describing appearance
units.topic: Words for describing beauty, size, hair and physical appearance

words.description: The way someone or something looks
words.example: Your appearance is the way you look.

words.description: Pleasing in appearance
words.example: Women can be attractive or good-looking.

words.description: Nice to look at; attractive
words.example: Women can be attractive or good-looking.

words.description: Attractive, especially used for a girl
words.example: We often use pretty to describe a girl.

words.description: Extremely beautiful or attractive
words.example: We use gorgeous for women who are very attractive.

words.description: Attractive, especially used for men
words.example: Liam has become quite handsome.

words.description: Not special or different; a polite alternative to 'ugly'
words.example: Ordinary [not special or different] is more polite than ugly.

words.description: How tall or short a person is
words.example: We can talk about a person's height.

words.description: How heavy a person is
words.example: We can talk about their weight.

words.description: Used to indicate a number is not exact
words.example: I'm roughly one metre eighty (tall).

words.description: Neither tall nor short
words.example: If someone is not tall or short, you can describe them as medium height.

words.description: Typical; similar to most other people
words.example: If a person is very similar to most other people in height and weight, you can say they are average.

words.description: Having light yellow coloured hair
words.example: His hair is blonde (or blond).

words.description: Light coloured hair, lighter than brown
words.example: Her hair colour is fair.

words.description: Hair with gentle curves; between straight and curly
words.example: Her hair is wavy, not straight or curly.

words.description: Hair with tight curls
words.example: Her hair is curly.

words.description: A question asking someone to describe a person's appearance
words.example: What does Sophia's boyfriend look like?

words.description: Quite tall; using the suffix -ish meaning 'quite'
words.example: He's tallish, but he's got broad shoulders.

words.description: Wide shoulders; the opposite of narrow shoulders
words.example: He's got broad shoulders. [wide; opposite narrow]

words.description: Strong, healthy and good at sports
words.example: He looks very athletic.

words.description: Clean, tidy and stylish in appearance
words.example: Is he quite smart? [clean, tidy and stylish]

words.description: To wear nice, fashionable clothes
words.example: He dresses quite well. [the clothes he wears are quite nice]

units.title: Describing character
units.topic: Adjectives for describing personality and character

words.description: The particular combination of qualities a person has
words.example: What are you like as a person? Tell me about your character.

words.description: Believing that good things will happen
words.example: I think I'm very positive.

words.description: Not believing that good things will happen; opposite of positive
words.example: I'm quite a negative person.

words.description: Can be trusted to do what people expect you to do
words.example: I'm usually reliable.

words.description: Cannot be trusted; opposite of reliable
words.example: I'm quite unreliable.

words.description: Feeling sure about yourself and your abilities
words.example: I'm quite confident.

words.description: Not confident, especially about meeting or talking to new people
words.example: I'm quite shy.

words.description: Putting a lot of effort into your work and spending a lot of time on it
words.example: I'm hard-working.

words.description: Not wanting to work or be active; opposite of hard-working
words.example: I'm quite lazy.

words.description: The ability to laugh and understand when something is funny
words.example: I have a good sense of humour.

words.description: Quiet and doesn't laugh very much; opposite of a good sense of humour
words.example: I'm usually quite serious.

words.description: Able to stay calm and not get angry, especially when things take a lot of time
words.example: I'm usually quite patient.

words.description: Not able to wait calmly; opposite of patient
words.example: I'm quite impatient.

words.description: Happy to give more money or help than is usual
words.example: She's very generous — she always pays for everyone.

words.description: Not willing to give or spend money; opposite of generous
words.example: He's so mean — he never buys anyone a coffee.

words.description: An honest person tells the truth
words.example: She's always honest with me.

words.description: Not honest; tells lies; opposite of honest
words.example: He's dishonest — I don't trust him.

words.description: Able to learn and understand things quickly
words.example: She's very clever / intelligent.

words.description: Not intelligent; opposite of clever
words.example: Don't be stupid!

words.description: Relaxed and not worried or frightened
words.example: She's always calm in a crisis.

words.description: Worried or frightened; opposite of calm
words.example: I'm nervous about the exam.

words.description: Wants to help people a lot
words.example: She's a very kind person.

words.uzbek: do'stona, yoqimli, iliqlik bilan muomala qiladigan
words.description: Happy to talk to people; easy to like
words.example: She's very friendly and pleasant.

words.description: Practical; doesn't do stupid things
words.example: She's very sensible — I've never known her to do anything silly.

words.description: Good at thinking of new ideas and using imagination
words.example: She's also very creative; she makes things.

words.description: Having a natural ability to do something well
words.example: She's a very talented artist.

words.description: To believe that someone is honest and will not harm you
words.example: I know I can always trust her.

units.title: Feelings
units.topic: Emotions, feelings and how the environment affects mood

words.description: A strong feeling such as love, hate or anger
words.example: Love, hate and anger are emotions.

words.description: Showing feelings easily
words.example: Timo is a very emotional person.

words.description: Emotions; often used in the plural
words.example: She doesn't like talking about her feelings.

words.description: Feeling good because you or someone you know has done something well
words.example: I'm very proud of my son's success.

words.description: The feeling of being proud
words.example: She looked at her son with pride.

words.description: Unhappy because someone or something was not as good as you hoped
words.example: I'm a bit disappointed that the local paper hasn't shown more interest.

words.uzbek: umid puchga chiqishi, hafsalasi pir bo'lish
words.description: The feeling of being disappointed
words.example: She couldn't hide her disappointment.

words.description: Feeling positive about a future situation
words.example: I'm not very hopeful that things will improve.

words.description: Not able to think clearly or understand something
words.example: The politicians seem confused about what to do.

words.description: The state of being confused
words.example: There was a lot of confusion about the new rules.

words.description: Wanting to know or learn about something
words.example: We were curious to see what all the noise was about.

words.description: The feeling of wanting to know or learn about something
words.example: His curiosity led him to investigate further.

words.description: Worried about something that might happen
words.example: I felt anxious when I saw how angry the men were.

words.description: The feeling of being anxious or worried
words.example: She felt a sense of anxiety.

words.description: Afraid; synonym frightened
words.example: I felt really scared when they started coming towards us.

words.description: Unhappy and angry because someone you love seems too interested in another person
words.example: His girlfriend was getting jealous.

words.description: The feeling of being jealous
words.example: Jealousy destroyed their relationship.

words.description: Unhappy because something unpleasant has happened
words.example: Now he's quite upset.

words.description: Happy and positive; synonym happy
words.example: People say they feel more cheerful when the sun shines.

words.description: Unhappy; opposite of cheerful
words.example: People feel miserable when it's raining.

words.description: Unhappy, often for a long time, and without hope for the future
words.example: Dark winters can make some people feel depressed.

words.description: The way you feel at a particular time
words.example: Can the weather really affect our mood?

words.description: Wanting to be busy and doing a lot of things
words.example: Red can make us feel energetic.

words.description: Feelings of worry caused by difficult situations such as problems at work
words.example: Green is good for people suffering from stress.

units.title: Family and friends
units.topic: Family relationships, friends and social connections

words.description: Members of your family; synonyms
words.example: Relatives or relations means members of your family.

words.description: A woman whose husband has died
words.example: My mother was a widow with four young children.

words.description: To marry again after being divorced or widowed
words.example: She remarried five years later.

words.description: A man who has married your mother but is not your biological father
words.example: Now I have a stepfather.

words.description: Older — used for brothers and sisters
words.example: I've got an elder brother called Thomas.

words.description: Two children born to one mother at the same time
words.example: I have two younger sisters, Anya and Claudia, who are twins.

words.description: A family who like each other and stay together a lot
words.example: We're a close family.

words.description: A child without brothers or sisters
words.example: My mother is an only child.

words.description: To have a good relationship with someone
words.example: I get on well with Uncle Rolf.

words.description: The son of your brother or sister
words.example: He always tells me I'm his favourite nephew.

words.description: The daughter of your brother or sister
words.example: Of his nieces, I think he likes Anya best.

words.description: The wife of your brother, or the sister of your husband/wife
words.example: I now have a sister-in-law as well.

words.uzbek: eng yaqin do'st, eng yaxshi do'st
words.description: The person who is your closest friend
words.example: My best friend is Florian, an old school friend.

words.description: To gradually become familiar and friendly with someone
words.example: We got to know each other when we were in the same class at school.

words.description: Friends; an informal word for friends
words.example: We've been mates ever since.

words.description: From that time until now
words.example: We've been mates ever since.

words.description: The relationship between friends
words.example: Our friendship is very important to both of us.

words.description: Happening or existing now
words.example: His current girlfriend is actually one of Thomas's ex-girlfriends.

words.uzbek: sobiq qiz do'sti / sobiq yigit do'sti
words.description: A romantic partner from the past
words.example: She is one of Thomas's ex-girlfriends.

units.title: Growing up
units.topic: Ages, stages of life, and vocabulary about childhood and growing up

words.description: The moment a baby is born
words.example: Birth is the moment a baby is born.

words.description: A very young child, up to 12 months old
words.example: She had a baby last year.

words.description: A young child between 12 months and 3 years who is just learning to walk
words.example: My nephew is a toddler now.

words.description: The period when you are a child, from 3 to 12 years
words.example: She had a very different childhood.

words.description: A person aged approximately 13 to 18
words.example: He became a teenager last year.

words.description: The years of your life from 13 to 19
words.example: She died when I was in my teens.

words.description: A person who is fully grown up, aged 18 or above
words.example: You're an adult now — you can make your own decisions.

words.description: In the middle of your life, approximately 40 and above
words.example: People are middle-aged when they are in the middle of their lives.

words.description: When people stop work, usually at age 60 or 65
words.example: He is looking forward to retirement.

words.description: Having stopped work permanently
words.example: My parents are both retired now.

words.description: The later part of life, usually from about 80 onwards
words.example: We usually describe people over 80 as elderly.

words.description: A polite way to describe old people
words.example: We usually describe people as elderly when they are 80+.

words.description: Aged approximately 21-23
words.example: I'm in my early twenties.

words.description: Aged approximately 54-56
words.example: My parents are in their mid-fifties.

words.description: Aged approximately 77-79
words.example: My grandmother is in her late seventies.

words.description: Not quite; very close to a number or time
words.example: My grandfather is nearly eighty.

words.description: About; more or less
words.example: My English teacher's approximately thirty.

words.description: To be looked after until you are an adult
words.example: She was brought up on a farm in Wales.

words.description: Expecting people to obey rules or authority
words.example: Her parents were very strict.

words.description: To give permission to someone
words.example: They didn't allow her to listen to the radio.

words.description: To move out of your parents' home to live independently
words.example: She decided to leave home and get a job.

words.description: To gradually develop from a child to an adult
words.example: She grew up in the city.

words.description: To allow someone to do something; slightly more informal than 'allow'
words.example: Her parents let her stay out late.

words.uzbek: kech qaytmoq, kech vaqtgacha tashqarida qolmoq
words.description: To not go home until very late at night
words.example: Her parents let her stay out late.

units.title: Romance, marriage and divorce
units.topic: Relationships, marriage ceremonies and divorce

words.description: A planned romantic meeting
words.example: I had my first date when I was 16.

words.description: To have a romantic relationship with someone
words.example: I went out with a girl for three months.

words.description: When a romantic relationship ends
words.example: We broke up when she met a boy who was two years older.

words.description: A romantic or personal connection between two people
words.example: My first serious relationship was when I went to university.

words.description: To gradually become familiar with someone
words.example: I got to know Melanie because we were on the same course.

words.description: Having strong romantic feelings for someone
words.example: After a few months we realised we were in love.

words.description: To formally agree to marry someone
words.example: We got engaged a couple of years after we left university.

words.description: The period of time when two people are engaged to marry
words.example: Their engagement lasted two years.

words.description: Two, or perhaps three
words.example: We got engaged a couple of years after university.

words.description: An important public event with formal rituals
words.example: We didn't want a big ceremony.

words.description: The ceremony when people get married
words.example: We had the wedding in the local church.

words.description: The wedding party after the ceremony
words.example: We had the reception in a small hotel nearby.

words.description: A holiday just after getting married
words.example: We went on our honeymoon to Greece.

words.description: When a woman is going to have a baby
words.example: Just over three years later Melanie got pregnant.

words.description: The period of time when a woman is pregnant
words.example: The pregnancy lasted nine months.

words.description: A day that is exactly one or more years after an important event
words.example: Our first child was born just two days after our fourth wedding anniversary.

words.description: A time when you do something you enjoy because it is a special day
words.example: We had a big celebration.

words.description: To become a problem; to not work as planned
words.example: Things started to go wrong when I got a new job.

words.description: Without other people
words.example: She was often alone / on her own with two young children.

words.description: To stop doing something
words.example: I felt I couldn't give up my job.

words.description: When a marriage officially ends
words.example: The following year we got divorced.

words.description: The legal end of a marriage
words.example: Divorce can be very hard for children.

words.description: A woman on her wedding day
words.example: The bride wore a beautiful white dress.

words.description: A man on his wedding day
words.example: The groom waited nervously at the altar.

units.title: Daily routines
units.topic: Sleep, food, bathroom routines, housework and spare time

words.description: To stop sleeping
words.example: During the week I usually wake up about 7.30 am.

words.description: To get out of bed
words.example: I get up a few minutes later.

words.description: To start sleeping
words.example: I go to sleep straight away.

words.description: Immediately; without delay
words.example: I go to sleep straight away.

words.description: To go to bed very late
words.example: If I have a late night I try to have a sleep in the afternoon.

words.description: To go to bed early; opposite of have a late night
words.example: I'm tired, so I'm going to have an early night.

words.description: A food made from grain, eaten with milk for breakfast
words.example: I have coffee and cereal for breakfast.

words.description: A small or simple meal at lunchtime
words.example: I have a light lunch, maybe a sandwich and an apple.

words.description: A small amount of food eaten between meals
words.example: I have a snack in the afternoon.

words.description: The biggest and most important meal of the day
words.example: We have our main meal in the evening.

words.description: To make the effort to do something; often used in the negative
words.example: If Mum's late, she doesn't bother to cook.

words.description: A meal you buy in a restaurant but eat at home
words.example: We just get a takeaway instead.

words.description: To give food to a person or animal
words.example: One of us has to feed the cat as well.

words.description: To wash your face and hands
words.example: I only have time to have a wash.

words.description: To clean your teeth with a toothbrush
words.example: I clean my teeth (or brush my teeth).

words.description: To apply cosmetics to your face
words.example: Rosie comes in to put on a bit of make-up.

words.description: To shave your face
words.example: Marcus is having a shave.

words.description: The work of keeping a home clean and tidy
words.example: We've got a cleaner who does a lot of the housework.

words.description: A person who cleans houses or offices professionally
words.example: We've got a cleaner who does a lot of the housework.

words.description: To straighten and tidy the covers on your bed after sleeping
words.example: I still have to make my bed.

words.description: To iron clothes to remove creases
words.example: I have to do some of my ironing.

words.description: To buy food and essentials at the supermarket
words.example: I sometimes do the shopping with Mum.

words.description: To go to shops as a leisure activity, buying clothes, books, etc.
words.example: When we go shopping, it is a leisure activity.

words.description: Free time when you are not working
words.example: In my spare time I watch TV.

words.description: To remain at home rather than going out
words.example: On weekdays I usually stay in and watch TV.

words.description: To eat in a restaurant rather than at home
words.example: I eat out once a week.

words.description: To visit someone in their home
words.example: Sometimes friends come round.

words.description: To have an informal conversation
words.example: We chat about clothes, music and college.

units.title: The place where you live
units.topic: Location, types of accommodation and describing where you live

words.description: The place and position of something
words.example: We live in a very nice location.

words.description: On the edge of a town or city
words.example: We live on the outskirts of town.

words.description: To change the place where you live
words.example: We moved to our present flat when we had children.

words.description: An area that is empty or not used
words.example: There's more space for them to play.

words.description: The things you can see from a place
words.example: It has nice views.

words.description: To pay money every week or month to use a place that belongs to someone else
words.example: We rent a flat on the second floor.

words.description: A set of rooms for living in, usually on one floor of a building; called 'apartment' in American English
words.example: We rent a flat on the second floor.

words.description: A flat; used in American English and becoming more common in British English
words.example: Apartment is used in American English.

words.description: On a lower level of a building
words.example: There's a family in the flat downstairs.

words.description: On a higher level of a building
words.example: A young French couple lives upstairs.

words.uzbek: ko'p qavatli turar joy, kvartiralar binosi
words.description: A building with a number of flats in it
words.example: It's a modern block of flats.

words.description: A system that keeps the air cool inside a building
words.example: There's no air conditioning.

words.description: To have something that belongs to you because you bought it
words.example: My parents own a cottage.

words.description: A small house, usually old and attractive, found in the country
words.example: My parents own a cottage.

words.description: Pleasant and attractive
words.example: It's a charming house.

words.description: When a building is interesting and unusual
words.example: It has lots of character.

words.description: A system that heats a whole building
words.example: It doesn't have central heating.

units.title: Around the home
units.topic: Rooms, furniture and objects found around the home

words.description: To use something at the same time as someone else
words.example: She had to share our bedroom.

words.description: A bathroom connected directly to a bedroom
words.example: Our bedroom has an en-suite bathroom.

words.description: An extra room used for guests to sleep in
words.example: We have a spare room for guests.

words.description: A room where people can work or read
words.example: Downstairs, there's a living room, a dining room and a study.

words.description: A combined refrigerator and freezer
words.example: We have a lovely big kitchen with a fridge-freezer.

words.description: A machine for washing dishes automatically
words.example: We have a dishwasher.

words.description: A small room next to the kitchen for the washing machine and other appliances
words.example: There's a small utility room where we keep the washing machine.

words.description: A device that controls the flow of water
words.example: The tap is in the kitchen above the sink.

words.description: A container used to boil water for making hot drinks
words.example: She filled the kettle to make tea.

words.description: An oven that heats food very quickly
words.example: Heat the food in the microwave.

words.description: Flat pieces of ceramic used to cover floors or walls
words.example: The bathroom has tiles on the floor.

words.description: A long comfortable seat for two or more people with a back and arms
words.example: Some people like big soft sofas with lots of cushions.

words.description: Soft pads used for comfort on sofas or chairs
words.example: Some people like sofas with lots of cushions.

words.description: Not soft when you press it; opposite of soft
words.example: Some people prefer firm armchairs.

words.description: A floor made of wood
words.example: Do you like a wooden floor or a carpet?

words.description: A small carpet that covers only part of a floor
words.example: Do you like a wooden floor with a rug?

words.description: Fabric hanging at windows to block light
words.example: Do you prefer curtains or blinds?

words.description: A covering for a window made of strips of plastic or wood
words.example: Do you prefer curtains or blinds?

words.uzbek: yumshoq ko'rpa (paxta yoki past bilan to'ldirilgan)
words.description: A thick warm cover for a bed filled with feathers or other material
words.example: Do you prefer a duvet or a sheet and blanket?

words.description: A large piece of thin fabric used on a bed
words.example: Some people prefer a sheet and a blanket.

words.description: A thick warm covering used on a bed
words.example: Some people prefer a sheet and a blanket.

words.description: A soft bag filled with feathers or other material for resting your head on
words.example: One pillow, or two?

words.description: A basin with a tap, used for washing. Usually called 'sink' in a kitchen and 'basin' in a bathroom
words.example: We usually talk about a sink in the kitchen but a washbasin in the bathroom.

units.title: Money
units.topic: Currency, managing money, financial problems and accommodation costs

words.description: The type of money used in a country
words.example: In the UK the currency is sterling; in America it is the dollar.

words.description: The currency of the UK (pounds)
words.example: In the UK the currency is sterling.

words.description: A piece of paper money
words.example: A ten-pound note, a twenty-euro note.

words.description: Small round pieces of metal used as money
words.example: Coins: fifty pence, a pound.

words.description: The smaller unit of British currency; 100 pence = 1 pound
words.example: Fifty pence (usually spoken as fifty p).

words.description: An account at a bank where you keep and manage your money
words.example: I've had a bank account for a few years now.

words.description: Having money in your bank account
words.example: I make sure my account is always in credit.

words.description: A machine where you can get money from your bank account using a card
words.example: I go to the cashpoint once a week.

words.description: Money in the form of notes and coins
words.example: I always have some cash with me.

words.description: Money you borrow that you must pay back later
words.example: I had to get a student loan to pay my fees.

words.description: Money you pay to use something or for a service
words.example: I had to get a student loan to pay my fees.

words.description: To use money badly or unnecessarily
words.example: I had to make sure I didn't waste money.

words.description: To have to pay back money to someone
words.example: By the time I finished my degree I owed a lot.

words.description: To repay money you borrowed
words.example: I don't have to pay it back until I get a job.

words.description: To receive money for the work you do
words.example: When I'm earning a reasonable amount of money.

words.description: To keep money regularly in order to buy something in the future
words.example: I'm saving up for a new laptop.

words.description: To have enough money to buy or do something
words.example: I'd love a car but I can't afford it.

words.description: A place where you live or stay
words.example: This year I'm renting a flat — accommodation with three friends.

words.description: Money you pay in advance for something you are going to use, returned when you finish
words.example: We had to pay one month's rent as a deposit.

words.description: Good in quality or amount for the price paid
words.example: It's quite good value for money.

words.description: A person who rents out a property to tenants
words.example: The landlord isn't charging us to use his garage.

words.description: To pay to use something for a short period of time
words.example: I hired a bike for the day.

units.title: Health
units.topic: Common illnesses, describing pain and serious medical conditions

words.description: When your throat is painful and uncomfortable
words.example: I've got a sore throat and a temperature.

words.description: A high body temperature indicating illness
words.example: I've got a sore throat and a temperature.

words.description: Influenza; a common illness with fever, aches and tiredness
words.example: That sounds like flu. You should see a doctor.

words.description: To lose blood from a wound
words.example: I've cut my arm; it's bleeding.

words.description: A strip of fabric used to wrap around a wound or injured body part
words.example: Put a bandage round it.

words.description: A sudden noisy release of air from the throat
words.example: I've got a terrible cough.

words.description: Something you take to treat an illness
words.example: Get some cough medicine.

words.description: Small solid pieces of medicine that you swallow
words.example: Take some tablets for the pain. Also called pills.

words.description: To feel like you want to vomit
words.example: I feel sick.

words.description: A continuous pain that is not very strong. Used with headache, backache, stomach ache
words.example: An ache describes pain that is not always strong but often continues.

words.description: A pain in your head
words.example: I've got a headache.

words.description: A pain in your stomach
words.example: Aria's got stomach ache.

words.description: A pain in your back that continues for some time
words.example: My dad suffers from backache.

words.uzbek: ... dan aziyat cheking, doimiy og'riq bilan yashаmoq
words.description: To often have the pain or difficulty of a health problem
words.example: My dad suffers from backache.

words.description: A feeling of physical discomfort; used for body parts other than head, back, stomach
words.example: I've got a pain in my shoulder/foot.

words.description: Causing pain
words.example: It's very painful.

words.description: To cause pain; used for stronger or more sudden pain
words.example: My throat hurts when I speak.

words.description: A person who stays in hospital for medical treatment
words.example: A person who stays in hospital is called a patient.

words.description: A medical procedure when doctors cut into the body; also called surgery
words.example: Many patients need an operation.

words.description: A special doctor who performs operations
words.example: Special doctors, called surgeons, cut into the body.

words.description: Medical operations; another word for an operation
words.example: Also called surgery.

words.description: A serious medical problem affecting certain parts of the body
words.example: Disease is used for serious medical problems, e.g. heart disease.

words.description: A serious or minor medical problem; also used for conditions affecting the mind
words.example: Illness is used for serious and minor medical problems, e.g. mental illness.

words.description: A sudden serious condition where the heart stops working properly
words.example: Heart attacks can happen very suddenly.
